---
title: "Cost Ratio Literature Review: R = C_I / C_V"
subtitle: "PubMed-based survey across VIMC diseases"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-fold: true
    embed-resources: true
---

## Overview

This document summarises cost data extracted from PubMed abstracts for 14 vaccine-preventable diseases listed by the Vaccine Impact Modelling Consortium (VIMC). The goal is to estimate plausible ranges of the cost ratio $R = C_I / C_V$ (infection cost per case divided by vaccination cost per person) used in the reactive vs. pre-emptive vaccination framework.

::: {.callout-note}
Cost data were extracted from **abstracts only** using regex heuristics. Many cost figures appear only in full-text articles. This survey is a starting point for manual curation, not a definitive extraction.
:::

## Summary by Disease

| Disease | Articles | Year range | Study types | Cost-per-case mentions | Vaccine cost mentions |
|---------|----------|------------|-------------|----------------------|----------------------|
| Cholera | 122 | 1986–2026 | other(14), COI(46), CEA(67), BIA(1), CBA(11) | 1 | 51 |
| COVID-19 | 177 | 2020–2026 | CEA(148), COI(35), CBA(20), other(4), BIA(2) | 0 | 12 |
| Typhoid | 112 | 1990–2025 | COI(52), CEA(68), BIA(1), CBA(10), other(2) | 16 | 9 |
| Dengue | 169 | 2000–2026 | CEA(67), COI(107), CBA(10), BIA(4), other(3) | 14 | 6 |
| Haemophilus influenzae type b | 118 | 1985–2025 | other(7), COI(39), CEA(69), CBA(28), BIA(4) | 0 | 40 |
| Rotavirus | 178 | 1980–2025 | CEA(138), COI(73), CBA(14), BIA(9), other(1) | 11 | 67 |
| Pneumococcal disease (PCV) | 184 | 1980–2026 | COI(61), CEA(145), CBA(14), BIA(5), other(3) | 0 | 11 |
| Human papillomavirus (HPV) | 195 | 2003–2025 | CEA(182), COI(26), BIA(5), CBA(7), other(1) | 1 | 22 |
| Japanese encephalitis | 57 | 1990–2025 | CEA(32), COI(26), CBA(7), other(2) | 2 | 12 |
| Malaria | 185 | 1991–2026 | COI(33), CEA(156), CBA(20), BIA(6), other(3) | 44 | 16 |
| Measles | 162 | 1978–2026 | CEA(97), other(13), CBA(29), COI(41), BIA(2) | 23 | 22 |
| Meningococcal meningitis | 164 | 1980–2026 | CEA(119), COI(50), CBA(8), other(5) | 7 | 28 |
| Rubella | 148 | 1976–2025 | other(10), CEA(87), CBA(43), COI(35) | 8 | 15 |
| Yellow fever | 48 | 1980–2025 | COI(20), CEA(27), CBA(4), other(3) | 0 | 19 |

## Cholera

**122 articles** with cost data (1986–2026). Countries: worldwide, US, global, India, China.

### Cost per case mentions

| Amount | PMID | Year | Context (excerpt) |
|--------|------|------|-------------------|
| US$1.72 | [31150406](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/31150406/) | 2019 | ratio-cost per case averted, cost per life saved and cost per DALY averted-for a single dose OCV. RESULTS: The mean cost |

### Key articles for review

- [Breastfeeding promotion and priority setting in health.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/10158457/) (1996, Health policy and planning). Types: CEA. Money mentions: 30.
- [Oral Cholera Vaccination Delivery Cost in Low- and Middle-Income Countries: An Analysis Based on Systematic Review.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/27930668/) (2016, PLoS neglected tropical diseases). Types: CEA. Money mentions: 16.
- [Evaluating the costs of cholera illness and cost-effectiveness of a single dose oral vaccination campaign in Lusaka, Zambia.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/31150406/) (2019, PloS one). Types: CEA. Money mentions: 14.
- [Potential impact of reactive vaccination in controlling cholera outbreaks: an exploratory analysis using a Zimbabwean experience.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/21920160/) (2011, South African medical journal = Suid-Afrikaanse tydskrif vir geneeskunde). Types: CEA, COI. Money mentions: 11.
- [Economic burden of cholera in Asia.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/31611097/) (2020, Vaccine). Types: COI. Money mentions: 12.

## COVID-19

**177 articles** with cost data (2020–2026). Countries: worldwide, us, Global, sub-Saharan Africa, global.

### Vaccine cost mentions

| Amount | PMID | Year | Context (excerpt) |
|--------|------|------|-------------------|
| US$7 | [35914832](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/35914832/) | 2022 | ising roll-out in those over 50-years (80% uptake in all scenarios). Cost data were obtained from primary analyses. We a |
| US$3.90 | [35914832](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/35914832/) | 2022 | ptake in all scenarios). Cost data were obtained from primary analyses. We assumed vaccine procurement at US$7 per dose  |
| US$6.11 | [35914832](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/35914832/) | 2022 | all scenarios). Cost data were obtained from primary analyses. We assumed vaccine procurement at US$7 per dose and vacci |
| US$919.11 | [35914832](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/35914832/) | 2022 | nalyses. We assumed vaccine procurement at US$7 per dose and vaccine delivery costs of US$3.90-US$6.11 per dose. The cos |
| US$2300 | [38824084](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38824084/) | 2024 | , respectively. In the PSA, almost 100% of simulations fell below the estimated opportunity cost-based cost-effectivenes |
| $11 | [36827972](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/36827972/) | 2023 | d use of fractional dosing for boosters, together with delivery costs divided by fractionation, could substantially gain |
| $2 | [36827972](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/36827972/) | 2023 | for boosters, together with delivery costs divided by fractionation, could substantially gain more net monetary benefit  |
| US$318, | [36273987](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/36273987/) | 2022 | d to the current vaccination programme, PCV20 vaccination programme gained more QALYs with less cost, while PCV15 vaccin |
| US$1 | [36273987](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/36273987/) | 2022 | current vaccination programme, PCV20 vaccination programme gained more QALYs with less cost, while PCV15 vaccination pro |
| US$11 | [40645638](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40645638/) | 2025 | veness in terms of death prevention, reduction in medical costs and timely COVID-19 testing. RESULTS: The analysis revea |

### Key articles for review

- [Emerging Therapies for COVID-19: The Value of Information From More Clinical Trials.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/35490085/) (2022, Value in health : the journal of the International Society for Pharmacoeconomics and Outcomes Research). Types: CEA. Money mentions: 12.
- [Economic burden of public health care and hospitalisation associated with COVID-19 in China.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/35032917/) (2022, Public health). Types: CEA, COI. Money mentions: 12.
- [Cost-effectiveness of Low-complexity Screening Tests in Community-based Case-finding for Tuberculosis.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37623745/) (2024, Clinical infectious diseases : an official publication of the Infectious Diseases Society of America). Types: CEA. Money mentions: 11.
- [College Campuses and COVID-19 Mitigation: Clinical and Economic Value.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/33347322/) (2021, Annals of internal medicine). Types: CEA. Money mentions: 10.
- [Cost-effectiveness of interventions for the prevention and control of COVID-19: Systematic review of 85 modelling studies.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/35712857/) (2022, Journal of global health). Types: CEA, CBA. Money mentions: 9.

### Key articles for review

- [Measurement of the personal cost of illness due to some major water-related diseases in an Indian rural population.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/2351512/) (1990, International journal of epidemiology). Types: COI. Money mentions: 12.
- [Direct Medical Costs of 3 Reportable Travel-Related Infections in Ontario, Canada, 2012-2014.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/31310226/) (2019, Emerging infectious diseases). Types: CEA. Money mentions: 6.
- [Typhoid and Paratyphoid Cost of Illness in Nepal: Patient and Health Facility Costs From the Surveillance for Enteric Fever in Asia Project II.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/33258938/) (2020, Clinical infectious diseases : an official publication of the Infectious Diseases Society of America). Types: COI. Money mentions: 6.
- [Typhoid and Paratyphoid Cost of Illness in Bangladesh: Patient and Health Facility Costs From the Surveillance for Enteric Fever in Asia Project II.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/33258940/) (2020, Clinical infectious diseases : an official publication of the Infectious Diseases Society of America). Types: COI. Money mentions: 6.
- [Typhoid and Paratyphoid Cost of Illness in Pakistan: Patient and Health Facility Costs From the Surveillance for Enteric Fever in Asia Project II.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/33258941/) (2020, Clinical infectious diseases : an official publication of the Infectious Diseases Society of America). Types: COI. Money mentions: 5.

### Key articles for review

- [Cost of Dengue Illness in Indonesia across Hospital, Ambulatory, and not Medically Attended Settings.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/32901596/) (2020, The American journal of tropical medicine and hygiene). Types: COI. Money mentions: 18.
- [Cost-effectiveness of dengue vaccination in ten endemic countries.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/29229427/) (2018, Vaccine). Types: CEA. Money mentions: 11.
- [Costs of dengue to the health system and individuals in Colombia from 2010 to 2012.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/25667054/) (2015, The American journal of tropical medicine and hygiene). Types: COI. Money mentions: 9.
- [Cost-Effectiveness of Dengue Vaccination in Indonesia: Considering Integrated Programs with Wolbachia-Infected Mosquitos and Health Education.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/32545688/) (2020, International journal of environmental research and public health). Types: CEA. Money mentions: 9.
- [Economic report on the cost of dengue fever in Vietnam: case of a provincial hospital.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/28031723/) (2017, ClinicoEconomics and outcomes research : CEOR). Types: COI. Money mentions: 6.

## Haemophilus influenzae type b

**118 articles** with cost data (1985–2025). Countries: United States, Worldwide, worldwide, China, Thailand.

### Vaccine cost mentions

| Amount | PMID | Year | Context (excerpt) |
|--------|------|------|-------------------|
| US$3 | [19093776](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/19093776/) | 2009 | s evaluated, Hib vaccine was shown to be cost effective, although the vaccine is not yet cost saving based on pentavalen |
| US$ 6252, | [26044493](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/26044493/) | 2015 | l costs per DALY were below GDP per capita. RESULT: From the societal perspective, incremental costs per discounted case |
| US$ 26,476 | [26044493](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/26044493/) | 2015 | r DALY were below GDP per capita. RESULT: From the societal perspective, incremental costs per discounted case, death an |

| US$ 0.48 | [26044493](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/26044493/) | 2015 | US$ 0.69/dose. From the governmental perspective, the results were US$ 6954, US$ 29,449, and US$ 1373, respectively; the |

### Key articles for review

- [Achieving immunization agenda 2030 coverage targets for 14 pathogens: Projected product and immunization delivery costs for 194 Countries, 2021-2030.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/36686400/) (2023, Vaccine: X). Types: other. Money mentions: 12.
- [Economic evaluation of delivering Haemophilus influenzae type b vaccine in routine immunization services in Kenya.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/17768499/) (2007, Bulletin of the World Health Organization). Types: CEA. Money mentions: 12.
- [An economic analysis of alternatives for childhood immunisation against Haemophilus influenzae type b disease.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/7718653/) (1994, Australian journal of public health). Types: CEA. Money mentions: 11.
- [Estimating the economic impact of pneumococcal conjugate, Haemophilus influenzae type b and rotavirus vaccines in India: National and state-level analyses.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/31607600/) (2019, Vaccine). Types: COI. Money mentions: 12.
- [Cost-effectiveness of Haemophilus influenzae type b vaccine in Vietnam.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/26044493/) (2015, Vaccine). Types: CEA, COI. Money mentions: 9.

## Rotavirus

**178 articles** with cost data (1980–2025). Countries: China, worldwide, Mozambique, middle-income, Global.

### Cost per case mentions

| Amount | PMID | Year | Context (excerpt) |
|--------|------|------|-------------------|
| US$60 million | [29223486](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/29223486/) | 2018 | million cases and 8900 deaths while saving US$6 to US$9 million in costs over a 20-year period. The net cost of vaccinat |
| US$238 | [29223486](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/29223486/) | 2018 | over a 20-year period. The net cost of vaccination program is approximately US$60 million over the same period. The soci |
| US$332 | [29223486](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/29223486/) | 2018 | year period. The net cost of vaccination program is approximately US$60 million over the same period. The societal cost  |
| US$27 | [29223486](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/29223486/) | 2018 | m is approximately US$60 million over the same period. The societal cost per DALY averted is US$238 to US$332 with cost  |
| US$38 | [29223486](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/29223486/) | 2018 | oximately US$60 million over the same period. The societal cost per DALY averted is US$238 to US$332 with cost per case  |

### Key articles for review

- [Breastfeeding promotion and priority setting in health.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/10158457/) (1996, Health policy and planning). Types: CEA. Money mentions: 30.
- [Economic burden of measles outbreaks: a cost-of-illness study in a middle-income country in the post-elimination era.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39687243/) (2024, Revista panamericana de salud publica = Pan American journal of public health). Types: COI. Money mentions: 12.
- [Achieving immunization agenda 2030 coverage targets for 14 pathogens: Projected product and immunization delivery costs for 194 Countries, 2021-2030.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/36686400/) (2023, Vaccine: X). Types: other. Money mentions: 12.
- [Economic value of vaccinating geographically hard-to-reach populations with measles vaccine: A modeling application in Kenya.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/30922700/) (2019, Vaccine). Types: CEA. Money mentions: 12.
- [A Review of Measles Outbreak Cost Estimates From the United States in the Postelimination Era (2004-2017): Estimates by Perspective and Cost Type.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/31967305/) (2020, Clinical infectious diseases : an official publication of the Infectious Diseases Society of America). Types: CEA. Money mentions: 9.

## Meningococcal meningitis

**164 articles** with cost data (1980–2026). Countries: Global, Worldwide, United States, Europe, worldwide.

### Cost per case mentions

| Amount | PMID | Year | Context (excerpt) |
|--------|------|------|-------------------|
| $40,075 | [30763284](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/30763284/) | 2019 | .001) or visit the intensive care unit (49.2% vs. 2.4%; P < 0.001), and were associated with significantly higher mean h |
| $2827 | [30763284](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/30763284/) | 2019 | it the intensive care unit (49.2% vs. 2.4%; P < 0.001), and were associated with significantly higher mean hospitalizati |
| $9867 | [30763284](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/30763284/) | 2019 | 75 vs. $2827; P < 0.001). The hospitalization cost per case remained significantly higher when excluding the initial hos |
| $3312 | [30763284](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/30763284/) | 2019 | 27; P < 0.001). The hospitalization cost per case remained significantly higher when excluding the initial hospitalizati |
| US$59 | [11348706](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/11348706/) | 2001 | performed during 1997. The preventive strategy prevented 59% of the cases compared to 49% for the emergency strategy. Th |
| US$133 | [11348706](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/11348706/) | 2001 | strategy prevented 59% of the cases compared to 49% for the emergency strategy. The cost per case prevented was US$59 fo |
| US$0.20 | [11348706](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/11348706/) | 2001 | y strategy. The cost per case prevented was US$59 for the preventive strategy and US$133 for the reactive strategy, and  |

### Key articles for review

- [Potential impact of vaccination against Neisseria meningitidis on Neisseria gonorrhoeae in the United States: results from a decision-analysis model.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/25483706/) (2014, Human vaccines & immunotherapeutics). Types: other. Money mentions: 14.
- [Healthcare Resource Utilization and Cost of Invasive Meningococcal Disease in Ontario, Canada.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/30763284/) (2019, The Pediatric infectious disease journal). Types: COI. Money mentions: 10.
- [Treatment costs of long-term invasive meningococcal disease sequelae: A literature review and Delphi study in Brazil.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39978116/) (2025, The Brazilian journal of infectious diseases : an official publication of the Brazilian Society of Infectious Diseases). Types: other. Money mentions: 12.
- [The cost-effectiveness of alternative vaccination strategies for polyvalent meningococcal vaccines in Burkina Faso: A transmission dynamic modeling study.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/29364884/) (2018, PLoS medicine). Types: CEA. Money mentions: 10.
- [Cost-effectiveness Comparison of Monovalent C Versus Quadrivalent ACWY Meningococcal Conjugate Vaccination in Canada.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/28027288/) (2017, The Pediatric infectious disease journal). Types: CEA, COI. Money mentions: 7.

## Rubella

**148 articles** with cost data (1976–2025). Countries: United States, worldwide, middle-income, India, Zambia.

|--------|------|------|-------------------|
| US $9.9 million | [31107215](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/31107215/) | 2019 | period, 12,427 measles cases and 24,627 rubella cases were recorded; 27 infants had congenital rubella syndrome (CRS). T |
| US $439 | [31107215](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/31107215/) | 2019 | 4,627 rubella cases were recorded; 27 infants had congenital rubella syndrome (CRS). The cost of the outbreaks was US $9 |

| US $44,051 | [31107215](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/31107215/) | 2019 | had congenital rubella syndrome (CRS). The cost of the outbreaks was US $9.9 million. Cost per case was US $439 for meas |

| US$3.3 million | [15689089](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/15689089/) | 2004 | estimates of total cost represent approximately US$188 per varicella case per year. In comparison, the cost of a vaccina |
| US$104 | [15689089](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/15689089/) | 2004 | per varicella case per year. In comparison, the cost of a vaccination program was found to be US$3.3 million per annum.  |
| US$2 | [15689089](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/15689089/) | 2004 | r annum. The cost per case averted was US$104. From a societal point of view, for every dollar invested in a vaccination |

### Key articles for review

- [Achieving immunization agenda 2030 coverage targets for 14 pathogens: Projected product and immunization delivery costs for 194 Countries, 2021-2030.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/36686400/) (2023, Vaccine: X). Types: other. Money mentions: 12.
- [Economic burden of varicella in Singapore--a cost benefit estimate of implementation of a routine varicella vaccination.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/15689089/) (2004, The Southeast Asian journal of tropical medicine and public health). Types: COI, CBA. Money mentions: 5.
- [Estimated economic impact of vaccinations in 73 low- and middle-income countries, 2001-2020.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/28867843/) (2017, Bulletin of the World Health Organization). Types: COI. Money mentions: 7.
- [Assessment of Economic Burden of Concurrent Measles and Rubella Outbreaks, Romania, 2011-2012.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/31107215/) (2019, Emerging infectious diseases). Types: COI. Money mentions: 4.
- [Economic evaluation of second measles containing vaccine (MCV) dose: a systematic review of available evidence.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38924461/) (2024, Expert review of vaccines). Types: CBA. Money mentions: 3.

### Key articles for review

- [Achieving immunization agenda 2030 coverage targets for 14 pathogens: Projected product and immunization delivery costs for 194 Countries, 2021-2030.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/36686400/) (2023, Vaccine: X). Types: other. Money mentions: 12.
- [The Public Health Benefits and Economic Value of Routine Yellow Fever Vaccination in Colombia.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/30974312/) (2019, Value in health regional issues). Types: CEA. Money mentions: 7.
- [Burden of Chikungunya Fever and Its Economic and Social Impacts Worldwide: A Systematic Review.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40692505/) (2025, Tropical medicine & international health : TM & IH). Types: CEA, COI. Money mentions: 6.
- [Estimated economic impact of vaccinations in 73 low- and middle-income countries, 2001-2020.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/28867843/) (2017, Bulletin of the World Health Organization). Types: COI. Money mentions: 7.
- [Incremental costs of introducing jet injection technology for delivery of routine childhood vaccinations: comparative analysis from Brazil, India, and South Africa.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/21115059/) (2011, Vaccine). Types: other. Money mentions: 6.

## Methodology

Articles were retrieved via NCBI E-utilities (PubMed) using disease-specific search terms combined with economic/cost keywords. Cost data were extracted from abstracts using regex pattern matching for USD amounts. Study types were classified by keyword matching (CEA, COI, CBA, BIA). Countries/regions were identified by name matching.

**Limitations**: (1) Only abstracts searched — many cost figures are in full text only. (2) Currency conversion and inflation adjustment not applied. (3) Regex extraction is approximate — manual verification needed. (4) Non-English articles excluded.
